summary: Frontend refactoring test completed for OrderScreen.js. The extraction of ~244 lines of JSX to AccountSelectorLobby.js and SplitCheckView.js components is working correctly. All tested features function as expected with no import errors or console errors related to the refactoring.

issues: Minor: DialogContent accessibility warnings (missing Description/aria-describedby) - not blocking

AccountSelectorLobby.js.description: Grid of divided accounts for a table
features_tested: Displays when table has multiple orders (3 cuentas abiertas)
Shows account cards with totals and item previews
Nueva, Unir, Todas buttons visible and functional
Clicking account navigates to full OrderScreen

SplitCheckView.js.description: Split check mode for moving items between accounts
features_tested: Shows EDITAR CUENTA header with X close button
Nueva Cuenta and Mover a Cuenta buttons visible

login_test.result: Successfully logged in and navigated to dashboard

pre_cuenta_test.content: ALONZO CIGAR PRE-CUENTA with Mesa, Mesero, Fecha, items, ITBIS, Propina, Total

mobile_viewport_test.order_screen: Categories grid, mobile floating button (🛒 1 RD$ 200.00)

accessibility_warnings: DialogContent requires DialogTitle for screen readers
Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent

critical_code_review_comments: AccountSelectorLobby.js: Clean extraction with proper props (tableOrders, onSelectAccount, onMerge, etc.)
SplitCheckView.js: Clean extraction with proper props (selectedSplitItems, onToggleSplitItem, etc.)
OrderScreen.js: Imports both new components correctly at lines 16-17
No axios reference issue found - verifyManagerPin uses fetch API correctly (line 823)
Minor: Some DialogContent components missing accessibility attributes (not blocking)
context_for_next_testing_agent: OrderScreen.js refactoring verified. AccountSelectorLobby.js and SplitCheckView.js components extracted and working correctly. All features (login, tables, order screen, account selector, split check, pre-cuenta, facturar) functional on both desktop and mobile viewports.
